steps.title: Identify the key requirement
steps.content: The conclusion states that anatomical features affect dogs' ability to distinguish speech from nonspeech. A supporting finding must demonstrate that dogs with different head shapes actually differ in this discrimination ability.

steps.title: Match to the answer that shows this
steps.content:
The correct answer demonstrates that shorter-headed dogs show a smaller difference in brain activity between speech (Spanish or Hungarian) and nonspeech (scrambled) compared to longer-headed dogs, directly proving that head shape affects speech-nonspeech discrimination.

The other choices either compare within a single head shape, test language familiarity rather than speech-nonspeech, or confuse the comparison.

That confirms B is correct.
